title: audr Report (v1)
description: Wire-shape of `audr scan -f json` and `audr findings ls --format json`. Audr commits to this shape across the v1 line: new optional fields may land in minor releases, existing fields keep their names and types.

schema.description: URL identifying the schema this Report conforms to. Consumers fetch this URL to validate the rest of the document.

version.description: Version of the audr binary that produced this Report (e.g. "1.1.0").

generated_at.description: RFC 3339 timestamp when the scan completed.

findings.description: List of findings in this Report. May be filtered (see applied_filters) and may respect suppressions; the baseline_diff section uses the unsuppressed truth.

roots.description: Scan roots that produced this Report.

self_audit.description: Result of audr's own integrity check: "clean (cosign-verified)" | "clean (unverified)" | "TAMPERED" | "skipped".

warnings.description: Scanner coverage warnings (e.g., missing backends) that must be surfaced in every Report.

attack_chains.description: Narrative scenarios connecting two or more findings into a single attack path.

environment.description: Runtime detection result (bare-metal / container / VM / WSL). Present only when --runtime-info was used.

scan_mounts.description: Host-bound vs container-local classification of each scan root. Linux-only; present only when --runtime-info was used.

applied_filters.description: Set by `audr findings ls` when one or more filter flags were active. Absent on direct `audr scan -f json` output.

baseline_diff.description: Set by `audr scan --baseline=<prior.json>`. Lists which finding ids were resolved, are still present, or are newly introduced compared to the baseline scan.

Taxonomy.description: Honest classification of the finding's actionability.

FixAuthority.description: Who can fix this finding: the user (`you`), a vendored-plugin maintainer (`maintainer`), or an upstream third party (`upstream`). Only `you` findings are agent-fixable; `maintainer`/`upstream` require human action.

title.description: Audr-controlled one-line summary. Safe to render verbatim in prompts.

description.description: Audr-controlled prose explaining why the finding fires. Safe to render verbatim.

path.description: Absolute or repo-relative path the finding was detected at. Not a secret; safe to render verbatim.

match.description: The matched value, post-redaction. May contain attacker-controlled text — render only inside an injection-safe envelope.

context.description: Per-rule metadata (e.g. `source=betterleaks validation=valid entropy=4.31`). May contain attacker-controlled text from file content — render only inside an injection-safe envelope.

suggested_fix.description: Audr-controlled remediation hint. Safe to render verbatim.

dedup_group_key.description: Groups findings that represent the same underlying vulnerability across multiple paths. For OSV findings: `osv:<ecosystem>:<pkg>:<fixed>:<advisory-id>`.

secondary_notify.description: Maintainer hint when the finding's path lives in a vendored plugin tree (e.g. "vercel"). Renders a "report to <vendor>" link alongside the primary action.

AttackChain.description: Narrative correlation across multiple findings. Schema is permissive in v1; consumers should treat unknown fields as forward-compatible.

rule_id.description: Glob pattern (filepath.Match) used to filter findings by rule_id.

baseline_path.description: Path of the prior scan JSON file used as the baseline.

baseline_scanned_at.description: RFC 3339 timestamp of the baseline scan. Empty when the baseline file did not record one.

resolved.description: Finding ids present in the baseline but absent from the current scan. The agent fixed these.

still_present.description: Finding ids present in BOTH the baseline and the current scan. Not yet fixed.

newly_introduced.description: Finding ids absent from the baseline but present in the current scan. The agent introduced these — could be a regression caused by the fix.

suppressions_off.description: Always `true` in v1.1: the diff is computed against the unsuppressed scanner result so adding a rule to .audrignore cannot fake "resolved". Documents the security invariant.
